overview.tagline: Gateway to the George Washington Bridge
overview.description: Fort Lee is a densely populated borough in Bergen County perched atop the Hudson Palisades directly across from Upper Manhattan. Known as the birthplace of the American film industry and the western anchor of the George Washington Bridge, Fort Lee is a vibrant, culturally diverse community with a dramatic skyline of residential high-rises, a thriving Korean and Japanese dining scene, and unmatched proximity to New York City.
highlights: Western anchorage of the George Washington Bridge
Historic birthplace of the American motion picture industry
One of the most ethnically diverse communities in New Jersey
Palisades Interstate Park runs along its eastern cliffs
Major Korean-American cultural hub with vibrant restaurant scene

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Fort Lee underwent a borough-wide revaluation in recent years.
realEstate.recentTrends: Fort Lee's real estate market remains strong due to NYC proximity. High-rise condominiums dominate the market, with newer luxury buildings commanding premium prices. The market is popular with buyers seeking Manhattan views and short commutes.
neighborhoodNotes: The Palisade Avenue corridor features luxury high-rise condominiums with Hudson River and NYC skyline views
Main Street area offers a walkable downtown with shops, restaurants, and mid-rise apartments
The Coytesville section in the north has more single-family homes and a quieter suburban feel
GWB-adjacent areas experience heavier traffic but offer the most convenient bridge access

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by neighborhood — check the borough website for your schedule
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use sealed bags or covered containers not exceeding 50 lbs
Bulk item pickup is available by appointment

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream recycling is collected weekly. The borough participates in the Bergen County Utilities Authority recycling program.
details: Commingled recyclables (plastic #1-#7, glass, cans) collected in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected separately
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-soiled items
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Suez Water (now Veolia) and sewer service is managed through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills are issued quarterly by Veolia
Sewer charges appear on property tax bills
Water quality reports are published annually
Emergency water main breaks: call the DPW

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Fort Lee Building Department handles all construction, renovation, and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for any structural work, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC modifications
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board or Zoning Board of Adjustment
Certificate of Occupancy required for all new construction and changes of use
Applications can be submitted at Borough Hall

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Fort Lee manages several municipal parking facilities and metered street parking throughout the commercial district.
details: Municipal parking lots and garages available near Main Street
Resident parking permits available for designated areas
Metered parking enforced Monday through Saturday
Overnight street parking restricted in many areas — check signage

additionalServices.title: Senior Services
additionalServices.description: The Fort Lee Senior Center offers programs, transportation, and meals for residents aged 60+.
details: Located at the Jack Alter Fort Lee Community Center
Transportation to medical appointments and shopping
Daily hot lunch program
Exercise classes, trips, and social events

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Recreation Department runs youth sports leagues, summer camps, and community events.
details: Youth basketball, soccer, baseball, and swimming programs
Summer day camp for children
Adult fitness programs at the Community Center
Seasonal events including concerts and movie nights

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Fort Lee enforces noise restrictions to maintain quality of life in its dense residential areas.
keyRules: Construction work prohibited before 8:00 AM and after 6:00 PM on weekdays
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Saturday construction allowed 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM
Excessive noise from residential units (music, parties) subject to fines
Commercial deliveries restricted during late night/early morning hours

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties to borough standards, including landscaping and structural upkeep.
keyRules: Lawns must be kept trimmed — grass over 10 inches may result in violation
Snow and ice must be cleared from sidewalks within 12 hours of storm ending
No accumulation of trash, debris, or abandoned vehicles on properties
Building facades and structures must be maintained in safe condition
Vacant properties must be registered and maintained

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed annually and kept under control at all times.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed with the Borough Clerk's office
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Owners must clean up after their pets on public and private property
Rabies vaccination required for all dogs and cats
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Fort Lee has strict parking regulations due to its density and proximity to the George Washington Bridge.
keyRules: Alternate-side parking enforced on many residential streets for street cleaning
No overnight parking on borough streets without a resident permit in designated zones
GWB traffic overflow parking strictly prohibited in residential areas
Fire hydrant clearance of 10 feet required at all times
Handicapped space violations carry significant fines

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Fort Lee has a large rental population and enforces landlord-tenant regulations actively.
keyRules: Landlords must register rental units with the Borough
Rent control ordinance applies to buildings with three or more units built before certain dates
Certificate of Habitability required for all rental units
Security deposit limited to 1.5 months' rent per NJ state law
Tenants protected under NJ Anti-Eviction Act
rentals.source: Fort Lee Borough Code, NJ Landlord-Tenant Law

other.title: Short-Term Rentals
other.summary: Short-term rental regulations apply to platforms such as Airbnb and VRBO.
keyRules: Short-term rentals must comply with borough zoning ordinances
Registration may be required — check with the Building Department
Fire safety and occupancy codes must be met
Complaints about short-term rental properties may result in enforcement action

publicSchools.name: Fort Lee High School
publicSchools.notes: Offers AP and Honors programs, strong STEM and arts programs

publicSchools.name: Lewis F. Cole Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive middle school with gifted and talented program

privateSchools.name: Calvary Christian Academy
privateSchools.notes: Faith-based education with small class sizes

privateSchools.name: Madonna Cemetery & Mausoleum School (Holy Angels Academy nearby)
privateSchools.notes: Academy of the Holy Angels in nearby Demarest serves many Fort Lee families

whyMoveHere: Unmatched proximity to Manhattan via the George Washington Bridge and bus routes
Vibrant, diverse community with excellent Korean, Japanese, and international dining
Stunning Hudson River and NYC skyline views from Palisades-top residences
Access to Palisades Interstate Park for hiking and recreation along the cliffs
Strong public transit options with NJ Transit bus service to the Port Authority

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Bus / Car via George Washington Bridge
commuteToNYC.duration: 20-45 minutes depending on traffic and destination
commuteToNYC.notes: Multiple NJ Transit bus routes run from Fort Lee to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Midtown Manhattan. The George Washington Bridge provides direct car access to Upper Manhattan. Ferry service from nearby Edgewater is also an option.

costOfLiving.summary: Fort Lee's cost of living is above the national average, driven primarily by housing costs and property taxes, but offers relative value compared to Manhattan.

pros: Closest NJ community to Manhattan with direct GWB access
Incredibly diverse population and world-class dining options
Beautiful Palisades cliffs with parks and river views
cons: Heavy traffic congestion, especially around the George Washington Bridge
Limited single-family housing — mostly high-rise condos and apartments
Higher property taxes compared to some neighboring towns
Street parking can be difficult in commercial areas

restaurants.name: Sushi Kai
restaurants.description: Acclaimed omakase and sushi restaurant offering exquisite Japanese cuisine with fresh fish flown in from Tsukiji Market.

restaurants.name: So Kong Dong
restaurants.description: Beloved Korean restaurant famous for its bubbling soft tofu stew (soon tofu) and authentic Korean comfort food.

restaurants.name: Gamja Tang
restaurants.description: Popular spot for Korean pork bone soup and hearty traditional Korean dishes in a casual setting.

restaurants.name: Hiram's Roadstand
restaurants.description: A Fort Lee institution since 1932, serving deep-fried hot dogs and classic American roadside fare.

restaurants.name: Ventanas
restaurants.description: Upscale Mediterranean restaurant featuring panoramic views of the Hudson River and Manhattan skyline.

parks.name: Palisades Interstate Park — Fort Lee Historic Park
parks.description: A scenic park atop the Palisades cliffs with Revolutionary War history, hiking trails, and sweeping views of the George Washington Bridge and Manhattan skyline.

parks.name: Constitution Park
parks.description: Community park featuring playgrounds, sports courts, a walking path, and picnic areas in the heart of Fort Lee.

parks.name: Ross Dock Picnic Area
parks.description: Riverside picnic area at the base of the Palisades cliffs, accessible by car, offering grills, restrooms, and waterfront views.
parks.address: Ross Dock Road, Fort Lee, NJ 07024

parks.name: Monument Park
parks.description: A small memorial park dedicated to local veterans, featuring monuments and a peaceful green space.

events.name: Fort Lee Film Festival
events.description: Annual celebration of Fort Lee's legacy as the birthplace of American cinema, featuring film screenings, lectures, and exhibits.

events.name: Fort Lee Day
events.description: Borough-wide community celebration with live music, food vendors, carnival rides, and fireworks.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual tree lighting ceremony in the downtown area with hot chocolate, caroling, and holiday festivities.

attractions.name: George Washington Bridge
attractions.description: One of the most iconic bridges in the world, connecting Fort Lee to Washington Heights, Manhattan. The pedestrian walkway offers spectacular views.

attractions.name: Fort Lee Historic Park
attractions.description: Part of Palisades Interstate Park, this site preserves Revolutionary War history where George Washington's troops retreated from the British in 1776.

attractions.name: Barrymore Film Center
attractions.description: A cultural arts venue housed in a restored Art Deco movie theater, honoring Fort Lee's film heritage with screenings, performances, and exhibitions.

shoppingAreas: Main Street — Fort Lee's walkable downtown with shops, banks, and restaurants
Lemoine Avenue — Korean and Japanese restaurants, bakeries, and specialty shops
Palisade Avenue — Mix of retail, dining, and services
The Shops at Hudson Lights — Modern mixed-use development near the GWB with retail and dining

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Thursday of each month at 7:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Fort Lee Borough Hall, Council Chambers, 309 Main Street, Fort Lee, NJ 07024

hospitals.name: Hackensack Meridian Health Palisades Medical Center
